static.message: Use z.input<> for input types (pre-transform) and z.infer<> or z.output<> for output types (post-transform). Mixing them causes silent type mismatches.
Prefer .safeParse() over .parse() at system boundaries. .parse() throws on invalid input, which can crash request handlers.
z.passthrough() allows unexpected fields through validation. Use .strict() for API input schemas to reject unknown fields.
z.coerce transforms input before validation (e.g., z.coerce.number() turns '' into 0). Ensure coercion behavior matches your intent.
